Search results: 



--- SOURCE 1: CRISPR Clinical Trials: A 2024 Update - Innovative Genomics Institute ---
URL: https://innovativegenomics.org/news/crispr-clinical-trials-2024/

SUMMARY:
It is a remarkable time for the development of CRISPR-based therapies. In late 2023, we saw the first-ever approval of CRISPR-based medicine: Casgevy, a cure for sickle cell disease (SCD) and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia (TDT) .



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 2: Advances in CRISPR-Cas technology and its applications ---
URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11669675/

SUMMARY:
This review explores the latest advancements in CRISPR-Cas technology and its therapeutic and biomedical applications, highlighting its transformative impact



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 3: Latest Developments in CRISPR and Genome Editing Clinical Trials ---
URL: https://www.iqvia.com/library/articles/latest-developments-in-crispr-and-genome-editing-clinical-trials

SUMMARY:
This article provides an update on current approved CRISPR and other gene editing therapies and outlines the latest development activities including ongoing



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 4: CRISPR Advancements for Human Health - PMC ---
URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11057861/

SUMMARY:
Recent clinical trials have demonstrated the potential of CRISPR-Cas gene editing to treat blood disorders like sickle cell disease (SCD) and beta-thalassemia.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 5: Advancements in CRISPR/Cas systems for disease treatment ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2211383525003132

SUMMARY:
This review provides a comprehensive overview of the development of CRISPR systems, vector technologies, and their applications in disease treatment



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://geneticliteracyproject.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/CRISPR.png
DESCRIPTION: A graphic outlines the evolution of CRISPR gene editing over the past decade, highlighting key advancements such as gene knockouts and sickle cell therapy, while also presenting future expectations like CRISPR-based treatments in clinical trials and increased nutritional value of crops.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://cdn.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/blobs/f351/10052255/508e8c6dd1ea/13346_2023_1320_Figa_HTML.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the components involved in CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing, including delivery systems like viral particles, polymers, peptides, lipids, and physical methods, combined with CRISPR/Cas9 systems such as Cas9 protein, mRNA, sgRNA, and RNPs, culminating in DNA editing.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://www.mdpi.com/ijms/ijms-24-07052/article_deploy/html/images/ijms-24-07052-g002.png
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates the process of CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing, showing the interaction of Cas9 with guide RNA to form a ribonucleoprotein (RNP), which then targets DNA for double-strand breaks and outlines pathways for gene disruption and addition through mechanisms like NHEJ, MMEJ, and HDR.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://ai2-s2-public.s3.amazonaws.com/figures/2017-08-08/4582e5142a38dd39fb701060f73c7963011654b2/2-Figure1-1.png
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the progression of CRISPR technology across three generations: the first shows the basic CRISPR-Cas9 system for gene disruption and repair, the second depicts versatile base editors for precise nucleotide conversions, and the third features prime editors capable of advanced DNA editing including insertions, deletions, and modifications.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://www.mdpi.com/ijms/ijms-25-13321/article_deploy/html/images/ijms-25-13321-g001.png
DESCRIPTION: None

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: CRISPR technology has seen significant breakthroughs, including the approval of Casgevy for sickle cell disease, marking a pivotal moment in gene editing applications.

CRISPR's versatility enables its use in treating various conditions from genetic disorders to potential agricultural improvements, showcasing transformative impacts in both biomedicine and food security.
Internationally, researchers are increasingly engaged in CRISPR studies, driving innovation and collaboration across borders to unlock its full potential for health and agricultural solutions.
